element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Basic combat, establishes turn-based ATB system with visible gauge
Top-down perspective traversal by foot; establishes spatial navigation
Information gathering, inns for resting, shops for equipment

element_introduction_order.element: Crystal shards and Job System unlock
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Acquisition of 22 jobs (26 in GBA); character customization; ability points progression
Unrestricted overworld movement; facilitates endgame exploration

element_introduction_order.element: Specialized jobs (Blue Mage, Time Mage, Mime)

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Optional content unlocks as story progresses
Central story structure; sealing Exdeath; elemental control

typical_relief_triggers: New job acquisition via crystal shard
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical: exploration tension (random encounters) → town relief (safe haven, shop/inn) → story escalation (dungeons, crystal mechanics) → mid-to-late game intensification (airship unlocks endgame content). Job system acquisition gates provide regular progression rewards.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Side quests become available as story progresses; non-mandatory secondary objectives

difficulty_curve.early_game: Low difficulty: Freelancer class introduction, basic random encounters, town-based pacing allows resource acquisition before mandatory challenges
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate difficulty: Crystal acquisition and job system mastery unlocks; dungeon combat complexity increases; narrative escalation with Exdeath threat
difficulty_curve.late_game: High difficulty: Airship unlocks full overworld access; endgame boss encounters (Gilgamesh); 22-job mastery enables player-driven tactical complexity
notable_spikes: Specialized job system interactions (Blue Mage, Time Mage, Mime introduce high-complexity mechanics)

notable_level_patterns.description: Story beats tied to Four Crystals seal mechanic; each crystal acquisition unlocks new areas, jobs, and narrative escalation. Structurally segments overworld into thematic regions.
Two distinct worlds referenced; World 1 and World 2 divisions suggest geographic or narrative separation enabling extended progression
Movement options progression: foot → Chocobo → hydra ship → wind drake → airship. Each unlock corresponds to story progression and increases accessible play space
Random encounters provide Ability Points alongside experience; dungeon encounters structured to encourage job-switching and ability experimentation
